records.question: Do both documents present a contents page before the operative text begins?
records.notes: Both PDFs open with a contents page on page 2.

records.question: Do both texts add a later dedicated section for definitions or interpretation rather than keeping definitions only in the opening pages?
records.notes: CRS uses Section VIII and Annex 1; the partnership law uses Schedule 1.

records.question: Do both contents pages list exactly nine top-level divisions before the supplementary material?
records.notes: Section I-IX and Part 1-9 are both listed on the contents pages.

records.question: Do both documents expressly refer to outside governing material in the operative text?
records.notes: CRS refers to OECD explanatory material; the partnership law refers to the Operating Law.

records.question: How many top-level divisions are listed across the two contents pages if you count only Parts in the partnership law and Sections I-IX in the CRS regulations?
records.notes: Nine sections in each contents page, counted as 9 + 9.

records.question: How many supplementary headings are listed across those contents pages if you count Annex 1 in the CRS regulations and Schedules 1-2 in the partnership law?
records.notes: One annex in CRS plus two schedules in the partnership law.

records.question: What is the difference between the title-year numbers in '2018' and '2004'?
records.notes: The title-year gap is 2018 minus 2004.

records.question: Which statute is the regulations document that is cited as the Common Reporting Standard Regulations?
records.expected_answer: Common Reporting Standard Regulations
records.notes: Uses the CRS title to distinguish it from the partnership law.

records.question: Which statute is the law titled General Partnership Law 2004?
records.expected_answer: General Partnership Law
records.notes: The partnership law title and section 1 identify it directly.

records.question: Which institution is named as the administrator of the partnership law, contrasted with the reporting authority in the CRS regulations?
records.expected_answer: Registrar
records.notes: Section 7 of the partnership law names the Registrar as administrator.

records.question: What are the two supplementary headings unique to the General Partnership Law contents page?
records.notes: The partnership law has two schedules; the CRS text instead has an annex.

records.question: What is the explicit in-force date stated on the CRS regulations title page?
records.notes: The title page states the regulations are in force on 30 July 2020.

records.question: What exact enactment date is stated for both statutes in these PDFs?
records.notes: The PDFs only reference an Enactment Notice for the partnership law and do not reproduce exact enactment dates.

records.question: In one sentence, what is the main functional contrast between the two statutes?
records.expected_answer: The CRS regulations set reporting and due-diligence rules for financial accounts, while the partnership law governs formation, administration, and dissolution of general partnerships.
records.notes: A concise synthesis of the documents' different regulatory aims.

records.question: How do the documents differ in the way they package supplementary material?
records.expected_answer: The CRS text uses an annex for additional definitions, while the partnership law uses schedules for interpretation, defined terms, and fines.
records.notes: Requires comparing Annex 1 with Schedules 1 and 2.

records.question: Do both judgments arise from first-instance DIFC Court proceedings rather than appeals?
records.notes: Both documents are judgments of the DIFC Court of First Instance.

records.question: Do both decisions include a final section after the main order that explains the court's reasoning?
records.notes: Techteryx has a schedule of reasons and Bond has a schedule of reasons.

records.question: Do both cases end up granting the claimant some form of relief?
records.notes: Techteryx keeps injunctions and Bond receives a money judgment.

records.question: Compared with the single defendant in the Bond case, how many defendants are named in the Techteryx case?
records.notes: Techteryx sues four defendants; Bond sues one.

records.question: By how many days did the Bond trial exceed the Techteryx FRD hearing?
records.notes: Bond's trial ran four days; the Techteryx FRD hearing ran three.

records.question: How many of the Techteryx defendants are banks?
records.notes: Mashreq, Emirates NBD, and Abu Dhabi Islamic Bank are bank defendants.

records.question: Which judge issued the Techteryx order?
records.expected_answer: H.E. Justice Michael Black KC
records.notes: Named in the title and reasons pages of the Techteryx order.

records.question: Which judge issued the Bond judgment?
records.expected_answer: Justice Wayne Martin
records.notes: The Bond judgment title page identifies the judge directly.

records.question: Who is the single judge common to both judgments?
records.notes: The two judgments were issued by different judges, so there is no shared judge name to extract.

records.question: Name the assistant registrars who issued the two judgments.
records.notes: Each judgment names a different assistant registrar.

records.question: What is the date of the older of the two judgments?
records.notes: Bond is the earlier judgment; Techteryx is dated 2025-10-17.

records.question: What is the main difference in relief between the two decisions?
records.expected_answer: Techteryx gets continuing interlocutory injunctions and consequential directions, while Bond gets a final money judgment for AED 2,873,837.14 plus costs.
records.notes: Contrasts interim asset-preservation relief with a final monetary award.

records.question: How do the procedural postures differ?
records.expected_answer: Techteryx is still in post-judgment consequential proceedings over injunctions, disclosure, privacy, and costs; Bond has already reached final trial judgment on the merits.
records.notes: One case is interlocutory/consequential, the other is a completed trial judgment.

records.question: What common feature do both judgments share at the end of the orders?
records.expected_answer: Each ends with a registrar-issued direction to agree further draft orders or consequential steps rather than a fully self-executing final disposal on every issue.
records.notes: Both decisions leave follow-on drafting or consequential issues to be completed.

records.question: What arbitration institution finally resolved both disputes?
records.expected_answer: There is no information on this question in the provided documents.
records.notes: The documents do not name any arbitration institution as the final resolver of either dispute.

records.question: Do these Regulations include their annexes and may they be cited as the Common Reporting Standard Regulations 2018?
records.notes: Covers the title and citation rule.

records.question: Does the reporting requirement include identifying the currency in which each amount is denominated?
records.notes: Direct reporting requirement.

records.question: May a Reporting Financial Institution use a service provider to fulfill the reporting and due diligence obligations?
records.notes: Section II service-provider rule.

records.question: May the due diligence procedures for New Accounts be applied to all Pre-existing Accounts?
records.notes: Section II flexibility rule.

records.question: Is an account with a negative balance deemed to have a balance or value equal to nil?
records.notes: Account balance rule.

records.question: Must a Group Cash Value Insurance Contract cover 25 or more employees or certificate holders to qualify for the temporary non-reportable treatment?
records.notes: Group contract threshold.

records.question: Must a Broad Participation Retirement Fund be subject to government regulation and provide information reporting to tax authorities?
records.notes: One of the retirement-fund conditions.

records.question: Does a Reportable Jurisdiction Person include an estate of a decedent that was resident in a Reportable Jurisdiction?
records.notes: Section VIII definition.

records.question: Must the term Controlling Persons be interpreted consistently with the Financial Action Task Force Recommendations?
records.notes: Interpretive instruction in the definition.

records.question: Is a trust that is a Passive NFE excluded from being treated as a similar legal arrangement for the Passive NFE rule?
records.notes: Section IX carve-out for trusts.

records.question: Does the document define any reporting rule for cryptocurrency wallets?
records.notes: Negative candidate; no wallet-specific rule appears in the document.

records.question: What percentage of gross income makes a Custodial Institution's holding of Financial Assets count as a substantial portion of its business?
records.notes: Custodial Institution income threshold.

records.question: How many employees or certificate holders must a group contract cover?
records.notes: Group contract coverage threshold.

records.question: What is the single-beneficiary asset-right limit for a Broad Participation Retirement Fund?
records.notes: Broad Participation Retirement Fund condition.

records.question: How many participants must a Narrow Participation Retirement Fund have fewer than?
records.notes: Participant-count threshold.

records.question: Within how many days must overpayments above USD50,000 be refunded under the qualified credit card issuer rule?
records.notes: Refund deadline.

records.question: What percentage of gross income is used for the Investment Entity test in subparagraph A(6)(b)?
records.notes: Primary-income threshold.

records.question: What is the maximum aggregate amount payable to any employee, certificate holder, or beneficiary under the group contract rule?
records.notes: USD cap in the group contract safe harbor.

records.question: What percentage of a Narrow Participation Retirement Fund's assets may be held by nonresident participants?
records.notes: Nonresident asset cap.

records.question: After how many months does the new-business exception for an NFE stop applying?
records.notes: Start-up exception duration.

records.question: How many cryptocurrency reporting categories does the document specify?
records.notes: Negative candidate; no cryptocurrency categories are specified.

records.question: What body makes these Regulations?
records.expected_answer: Board of Directors of the DIFCA
records.notes: Regulation-maker identification.

records.question: Which organization’s explanatory material is integral to the Common Reporting Standard for these Regulations?
records.expected_answer: OECD
records.notes: Interpretive source.

records.question: What is the term for an account maintained by a Financial Institution that includes Depository Accounts and Custodial Accounts?
records.expected_answer: Financial Account
records.notes: Core defined term.

records.question: What is the term for an account that holds one or more Financial Assets for the benefit of another person?
records.expected_answer: Custodial Account
records.notes: Definition term.

records.question: What is the term for a contract under which the issuer agrees to make payments for a period determined by life expectancy?
records.expected_answer: Annuity Contract
records.notes: Direct definition.

records.question: What is the term for an insurance company that issues or is obligated to make payments with respect to Cash Value Insurance Contracts or Annuity Contracts?
records.expected_answer: Specified Insurance Company
records.notes: Entity classification.

records.question: What is the term for a fund established to provide retirement, disability, or death benefits to fewer than 50 participants?
records.expected_answer: Narrow Participation Retirement Fund
records.notes: Retirement-fund definition.

records.question: What is the term for a Financial Account maintained by a Reporting Financial Institution as of 31 December 2016?
records.expected_answer: Pre-existing Account
records.notes: Temporal definition.

records.question: Which four categories are included in the term Financial Institution?
records.notes: High-level definition list.

records.question: Which two parties can a Reporting Financial Institution rely on for self-certification under Section IX B?
records.notes: Self-certification sources.

records.question: Which three entities are listed in the first Non-Reporting Financial Institution category?
records.notes: Section VIII non-reporting list.

records.question: Which natural persons are included in the trust definition of Controlling Persons?
expected_answer: any other natural person(s) exercising ultimate effective control over the trust
records.notes: Trust-control definition.

records.question: What is the in-force date shown on the cover page?
records.notes: Document date.

records.question: On what date does a New Account mean an account opened on or after?
records.notes: New-account cutoff.

records.question: As of what date is a Pre-existing Account defined?
records.notes: Pre-existing-account cutoff.

records.question: By what date must a Qualified Credit Card Issuer implement the policy or refund rule?
records.notes: Implementation deadline.

records.question: What information must each Reporting Financial Institution report for every Reportable Account?
records.expected_answer: The account holder's identifying details, account number, reporting FI name/number, year-end balance or closure, and the relevant income or proceeds amounts, with currency identified.
records.notes: Section I reporting bundle.

records.question: When is an account treated as a Reportable Account?
records.expected_answer: Beginning on the date it is identified as reportable under Sections II through VII, and it is then reported annually in the following calendar year.
records.notes: Reporting trigger and cadence.

records.question: What must a Reporting Financial Institution establish, maintain, and document under Section II?
records.expected_answer: The due diligence procedures in Sections II through VII that are designed to identify Reportable Accounts.
records.notes: Section II framework.

records.question: When may the residence address test be used for a pre-existing individual account?
records.expected_answer: When the institution has a current residence address in its records based on Documentary Evidence; then it may treat the holder as resident in that jurisdiction.
records.notes: Residence-address shortcut.

records.question: What does the electronic record search look for in lower value pre-existing individual accounts?
records.expected_answer: Indicia such as a Reportable Jurisdiction address, telephone number, standing instructions, power of attorney, or a care-of/in-hold mail address.
records.notes: Section III indicia search.

records.question: What must a new individual account self-certification do?
records.expected_answer: It must establish the account holder's tax residence(s) and confirm the certification's reasonableness based on the account opening information.
records.notes: New individual account onboarding rule.

records.question: What does Account Holder mean?
records.expected_answer: The person listed or identified as the holder of the Financial Account; for cash value insurance or annuity contracts, the person entitled to access cash value or change the beneficiary, or otherwise the owner/persons with vested entitlement.
records.notes: Miscellaneous definition.

records.question: What does Documentary Evidence include?
records.expected_answer: A certificate of residence, valid ID for an individual, official documentation for an entity, or audited financial statements, third-party credit reports, bankruptcy filings, or securities regulator reports.
records.notes: Documentary-evidence list.

records.question: What does Reportable Jurisdiction Person mean?
records.expected_answer: An individual, entity, or decedent's estate resident in a Reportable Jurisdiction; entities with no tax residence are treated as resident where effective management is situated.
records.notes: Residence-based definition.

records.question: What does Controlling Persons mean for a trust?
records.expected_answer: The settlor(s), trustee(s), protector(s) if any, beneficiaries or classes of beneficiaries, and any other natural person(s) exercising ultimate effective control.
records.notes: Trust-control definition.

records.question: What does Financial Asset mean?
records.expected_answer: It includes securities, partnership interests, commodities, swaps, insurance contracts, annuity contracts, and related interests, but excludes a non-debt direct interest in real property.
records.notes: Asset definition summary.

records.question: What is the account-maintained rule for a Depository Account?
records.expected_answer: It is maintained by the Financial Institution obligated to make payments on the account, not by an agent.
records.notes: Account-maintenance rule.

records.question: What does the document say about cryptocurrency wallet reporting?
records.expected_answer: There is no information on this question in the provided documents.
records.notes: Negative candidate; canonical no-information answer.

records.question: Did the Court lift the privacy attached to these proceedings?
records.notes: Direct holding at the end of the privacy section.

records.question: Did the Court say all hearings and all judgments should be treated as public?
records.notes: The Court expressly treated hearings and judgments as public.

records.question: Did the Court require a schedule listing every document for collateral use?
records.notes: The Court said no schedule was necessary.

records.question: Did the Court accept Techteryx's request for permission to use documents in undefined civil proceedings in any jurisdiction?
records.notes: The Court refused that broad formulation.

records.question: Did the Court accept the proposed 7-day period for disclosure compliance?
records.notes: The Court said 7 days was impractical and too short.

records.question: Did the Court reserve the costs order until the substantive claims were decided?
records.notes: The Court declined to reserve costs.

records.question: Did the order mention an appeal to the Dubai Court of Appeal?
records.notes: No appeal to the Dubai Court of Appeal is mentioned.

records.question: How many hours did the parties have to agree drafts of the orders?
records.notes: The order required drafts within 48 hours.

records.question: How many days did the Court allow for disclosure compliance?
records.notes: The Court set a 28-day period for compliance.

records.question: How much did the Court deduct from costs for Techteryx's disclosure misstatement?
records.notes: The Court reduced costs by USD 10,000 for the disclosure issue.

records.question: What amount was ordered to be paid on account of costs?
records.notes: Half of the adjusted costs claim was ordered on account.

records.question: How many DMCC payments with unidentified beneficiaries were identified in Schedule B?
records.notes: Schedule B is described as identifying 106 payments.

records.question: How many employees were in DMCC's compliance team?
records.notes: The document gives no information about DMCC headcount.

records.question: Which judge issued the amended order with reasons?
records.expected_answer: H.E. Justice Michael Black KC
records.notes: The title page and opening order identify the judge.

records.question: Who was the Assistant Registrar named as issuing the order?
records.expected_answer: Hayley Norton
records.notes: Her name appears in the issuance block.

records.question: Who represented Techteryx at the hearing?
records.expected_answer: Mr David Holloway
records.notes: The introduction identifies Techteryx's counsel.

records.question: Who represented DMCC at the hearing?
records.expected_answer: Mr George McPherson
records.notes: The introduction identifies DMCC's counsel.

records.question: Which judge explored the public-hearing issue in Lumbini v Lotika?
records.expected_answer: H.E. Justice Sir Jeremy Cooke
records.notes: He is cited as the judge in Lumbini v Lotika.

records.question: Which judge's collateral-use analysis did the Court rely on in Byju's Alpha?
records.expected_answer: H.E. Justice Le Miere
records.notes: The reasons expressly rely on his decision.

records.question: Which banks were the Second to Fourth Defendants?
records.notes: The case title lists the three bank defendants.

records.question: Which counsel appeared for the parties at the hearing?
records.notes: The introduction names both counsel.

records.question: Which judges are cited in the reasons on privacy and collateral use?
records.notes: The privacy and collateral-use sections cite these judges as authorities.

records.question: What date was the finalised judgment issued?
records.notes: The order refers to the Judgment dated 16 September 2025.

records.question: What date did the consequential hearing take place?
records.notes: The hearing is stated to have taken place on 2 October 2025.

records.question: What date was the order re-issued?
records.notes: The issuance block records the re-issue date.

records.question: What word did the Court substitute for "fabricated" in paragraph 365?
records.expected_answer: created
records.notes: The slip-rule correction replaced the word in the Judgment.

records.question: What did the Court say about when the amendment application should have been made?
records.expected_answer: It should have been made when the judgment was circulated in draft.
records.notes: The Judge criticized the timing of the amendment request.

records.question: What did the Court say about DMCC's privacy objection based on reputational damage?
records.expected_answer: There was no evidence DMCC, as opposed to the ARIA Fund, provided highly regulated financial services.
records.notes: The Court rejected the reputational-damage argument.

records.question: What did the Court say about the allegations of fraud in relation to privacy?
records.expected_answer: They had already been made in the Hong Kong proceedings.
records.notes: The Court treated the allegations as already public.

records.question: What test did the Court apply to wider collateral use of disclosed materials?
records.expected_answer: Special circumstances, plus necessity, proportionality, and no unfairness or prejudice.
records.notes: This condenses the Byju's Alpha principles the Court adopted.

records.question: How did the Court characterize DMCC's proposed wider collateral-use wording?
records.expected_answer: It was too wide and too vague.
records.notes: The Court rejected the broad wording in Techteryx's draft order.

records.question: What did the Court say about who should swear the disclosure affidavit?
records.expected_answer: MWB should swear it, with FTI assistance if needed, and take responsibility for it.
records.notes: The Court rejected the idea that MWB should be excluded.

records.question: Why did the Court refuse to reserve the costs order?
records.expected_answer: Because the freezing order served the normal purpose of a freezing injunction, so the costs were not reserved.
records.notes: The Judge distinguished freezing injunctions from American Cyanamid injunctions.

records.question: What did the Court say about further proceedings if Techteryx needs to preserve assets?
records.expected_answer: Techteryx could apply ex parte in writing if further proceedings were proposed to preserve assets.
records.notes: The Court allowed a route for later targeted applications.

records.question: What did the Court say about any settlement agreement between the parties?
records.expected_answer: There is no information on this question in the provided documents.
records.notes: No settlement agreement is discussed in the document.

records.question: Who administers the Law?
records.expected_answer: Registrar
records.notes: Article 7 states the Law and related legislation are administered by the Registrar.

records.question: What body may make Regulations for the purposes of the Law?
records.expected_answer: Board of Directors of the DIFCA
records.notes: Article 58(1) identifies the rule-making body.

records.question: What does DIFCA stand for?
records.expected_answer: DIFC Authority
records.notes: Defined term in Schedule 1.

records.question: What court is established under Dubai Law?
records.expected_answer: DIFC Court
records.notes: Defined term in Schedule 1.

records.question: What term is used for the person appointed by decree of the Ruler pursuant to Dubai Law?
records.expected_answer: President
records.notes: Defined term in Schedule 1.

records.question: What is the defined term for a general partnership formed in the DIFC?
records.expected_answer: General Partnership
records.notes: Defined term in Schedule 1.

records.question: What is the defined term for a general partnership formed outside the DIFC and registered under Article 13?
records.expected_answer: Recognised Partnership
records.notes: Defined term in Schedule 1.

records.question: What is the term used for legislation made by the Board of Directors of the DIFCA for the purposes of the Law?
records.expected_answer: Regulations
records.notes: Defined term in Schedule 1.

records.question: Is the Law administered by the Registrar?
records.notes: Article 7 assigns administration to the Registrar.

records.question: Must a person carry on a General Partnership business in the DIFC only if the partnership is registered or recognised?
records.notes: Article 9 contains the registration prohibition.

records.question: Does each Partner have to sign the partnership agreement?
records.notes: Article 10 requires the agreement to be signed by all partners.

records.question: Can the Registrar refuse to register a General Partnership or any Partner without giving reasons and without court review?
records.notes: Article 12(5) states the refusal need not be reasoned and is not subject to appeal or review.

records.question: Unless all partners agree otherwise, can ordinary matters be decided by majority even though a change in the nature of the business requires unanimous consent?
records.notes: Article 17(c) distinguishes ordinary matters from changes to the business nature.

records.question: Does every Partner take part in management by default?
records.notes: Article 17(a) sets the default management rule.

records.question: Is a General Partnership required to appoint an auditor in every case?
records.notes: Article 20(7) makes an auditor optional unless the Regulations or the partnership agreement provide otherwise.

records.question: May a Partner use information, money, or property of the General Partnership for private purposes without agreement?
records.notes: Article 26 prohibits private use unless all partners agree.

records.question: Are Partners jointly and severally liable for debts and obligations incurred while they are Partners?
records.notes: Article 28(1) states the default liability rule.

records.question: Does an outgoing Partner remain liable for debts incurred after ceasing to be a Partner?
records.notes: Article 34(2) limits outgoing partner liability to pre-exit debts.

records.question: Does the Law require annual general meetings of the partners?
records.notes: Negative deterministic candidate; this meeting requirement is not stated in the document.

records.question: How many persons are needed at minimum to form a General Partnership?
records.notes: Article 8 defines a general partnership as a relationship between two or more persons.

records.question: For how many years must accounting records be preserved?
records.notes: Article 14(2)(b) and Article 18(2)(b) both require six-year preservation.

records.question: Within how many months after the end of the financial year must accounts be prepared and approved?
records.notes: Article 19(4) gives the six-month deadline.

records.question: Within how many days after approval must the accounts be filed with the Registrar?
records.notes: Article 19(5) gives the filing deadline.

records.question: How many days does Schedule 1 define as a week?
records.notes: Schedule 1 defines a week as seven days.

records.question: How many days does Schedule 1 define as a month?
records.notes: Schedule 1 defines a month as thirty days.

records.question: How many days does Schedule 1 define as a year?
records.notes: Schedule 1 defines a year as 365 days.

records.question: Over how many days is the United Arab Emirates Central Bank deposit-rate benchmark period stated?
records.notes: Article 38(c)(ii) refers to the ninety-day deposit rate.

records.question: What is the maximum fine in Schedule 2?
records.notes: Schedule 2 lists the maximum fine.

records.question: How many days does the Law allow for filing a partnership tax return?
records.notes: Negative deterministic candidate; no tax-return filing rule appears in the document.

records.question: Which examples are included in the definition of Document?
records.notes: Schedule 1 definition of Document lists the included examples.

records.question: Which bodies are defined as the Court and the DIFCA?
records.notes: Schedule 1 defines both entities.

records.question: Which legal categories are referenced in Schedule 1 as legislation in the DIFC?
records.notes: Schedule 1's legislation-in-the-DIFC section lists these references.

records.question: Which examples are included in the definition of Accounting Records?
records.notes: Schedule 1 definition of Accounting Records lists these examples.

records.question: What month and year is shown for the consolidated version on the cover?
records.expected_answer: March 2022
records.notes: Cover page states Consolidated Version No. 2 (March 2022).

records.question: What year appears in the short title citation of the Law?
records.notes: The short title is the General Partnership Law 2004.

records.question: What year is given in the definition of the Operating Law?
records.notes: Schedule 1 defines the Operating Law by its 2018 citation.

records.question: What must be included in a General Partnership registration application?
records.expected_answer: The General Partnership name ending with 'partnership', the registered office address in the DIFC, the nature of the business, and each partner's name and address.
records.notes: Article 12(2) lists the required application content.

records.question: What must a Recognised Partnership registration application set out?
records.expected_answer: Its name, its DIFC service address and authorised service person, the nature of the business, each partner's name and address, and any additional information required by the Registrar.
records.notes: Article 14(2) lists the required application content.

records.question: What do the accounting records need to do?
records.expected_answer: They must be sufficient to show and explain transactions, disclose the financial position with reasonable accuracy, and let the partners verify compliant accounts.
records.notes: Article 18(1) describes the purpose of the records.

records.question: What is the default management rule under Article 17?
records.expected_answer: Every partner manages, no new partner may join without unanimous consent, ordinary matters may be decided by majority, and the business's nature cannot change without unanimous consent.
records.notes: Article 17 combines three default governance rules.

records.question: What does Article 27 allow the General Partnership to do?
records.expected_answer: Indemnify any partner or other person, subject to the standards and restrictions in the partnership agreement.
records.notes: Article 27 grants an indemnification power.

records.question: What happens if a partner misapplies fiduciary money or property under Article 31?
records.expected_answer: The General Partnership must make good the loss.
records.notes: Article 31 imposes partnership liability for the misapplication.

records.question: What must a partner do with partnership information, money, or property for private purposes?
records.expected_answer: Not use it for a non-business purpose unless all partners agree.
records.notes: Article 26 states the private-use restriction.

records.question: What does Article 21 accountability cover?
records.expected_answer: Property, profit, or benefit derived in the conduct of the business or from using the partnership's property, name, or business connection.
records.notes: Article 21(1) describes the fiduciary accounting duty.

records.question: What is the effect of a written notice to a partner under Article 33?
records.expected_answer: It operates as notice to the General Partnership unless the notice is part of a fraud on the partnership by or with that partner's consent.
records.notes: Article 33 sets the notice rule.

records.question: When may a partner dissolve the partnership by written notice under Article 39A?
records.expected_answer: When the partnership was formed for an undefined time and no fixed adventure or undertaking, subject to Article 42 and any agreement between the partners.
records.notes: Article 39A(1) sets the notice-based dissolution route.

records.question: What must the Registrar be satisfied of before consenting to a voluntary dissolution?
records.expected_answer: That no outstanding liabilities, guarantees, or other legal obligations will remain after the intended dissolution.
records.notes: Article 42(2) states the consent condition.

records.question: What is the effect of dissolution on partner authority for winding up?
records.expected_answer: The partners' authority continues only as needed to wind up the affairs and complete unfinished transactions.
records.notes: Article 49 preserves authority for winding up only.

records.question: What must the Registrar publish for public viewing?
records.expected_answer: A reasonably current version of the register of current and past registrations on the DIFC website.
records.notes: Article 60(3) requires public web access.

records.question: What does the Law say about any requirement to appoint a company secretary for a General Partnership?
records.expected_answer: There is no information on this question in the provided documents.
records.notes: Negative free_text candidate; the Law does not address any company secretary requirement.

records.question: Did the court allow the claim?
records.notes: Directly stated in the dispositive order.

records.question: Did the court dismiss the counterclaim?
records.notes: Directly stated in the dispositive order.

records.question: Did the parties enter into the MEP Works contract on 26 January 2020?
records.notes: The contract date is stated in the contract summary.

records.question: Was the defects period 365 days?
records.notes: The appendix fixes the defects period at 365 days.

records.question: Was the finance charge on delayed payment 15% per annum?
records.notes: The contract appendix states the finance charge rate.

records.question: Did Bond abandon its prolongation costs claim at trial?
records.notes: The concession was made in closing and changed the shape of the case.

records.question: Did the judgment say that either party filed an appeal?
records.notes: Negative: no appeal is mentioned in the judgment.

records.question: What was the lump sum price of the MEP Works contract?
records.notes: Stated in the contract summary.

records.question: What was the price of the Fit-Out Works contract?
records.notes: Stated in the contract summary.

records.question: What was the daily liquidated damages rate for the MEP contract?
records.notes: Given in the appendix for clause 7.4.

records.question: What was the daily liquidated damages rate for the Fit-Out contract?
records.notes: Given in the appendix for clause 7.4.

records.question: What amount did Bond claim in the original Claim Form?
records.notes: This is the balance claimed at the start of proceedings.

records.question: How many days did the judgment give a party to appeal?
records.notes: Negative: the judgment does not state any appeal timetable.

records.question: Who delivered the judgment?
records.expected_answer: Justice Wayne Martin
records.notes: Named in the judgment heading.

records.question: Who issued the judgment as Assistant Registrar?
records.expected_answer: Delvin Sumo
records.notes: Shown in the issue block at the end of the order.

records.question: Who was counsel for the Claimant at trial?
records.expected_answer: Mr. Nathan Landis
records.notes: Listed in the counsel section.

records.question: Who was Bond's MEP Operations Manager?
records.expected_answer: Mr. Ajay Kumar
records.notes: Identified in the witness section.

records.question: Who was TR88's co-owner and manager?
records.expected_answer: Dr James Khalili
records.notes: Identified in the witness section.

records.question: Which two witnesses of fact did Bond call?
records.notes: Both Bond factual witnesses are named in the judgment.

records.question: Which two witnesses of fact did TR88 call?
records.notes: Both TR88 factual witnesses are named in the judgment.

records.question: Which two expert witnesses gave delay and quantum evidence?
records.notes: Requires combining the expert-evidence section.

records.question: On what date was the judgment issued?
records.notes: The judgment date is stated at the top and in the issue block.

records.question: On what date did Bond commence these proceedings?
records.notes: The commencement date is expressly stated.

records.question: On what date did TR88 take possession of the premises and open them for business?
records.notes: Stated in the summary of completion and handover.

records.question: What reason did the court give for dismissing TR88's liquidated damages and lost profits claims?
records.expected_answer: Bond was not shown to be solely responsible for the delay; the evidence pointed to concurrent delay.
records.notes: The dismissal turned on failure to prove sole responsibility.

records.question: Why did the court accept Bond's AED 161,404.98 credit for the descoped roof-top MEP works?
records.expected_answer: Bond produced a detailed calculation and TR88 produced no contrary evidence to show a greater credit was due.
records.notes: The court accepted Bond's uncontradicted calculation.

records.question: Why were TR88's back charges for third-party rectification works rejected?
records.expected_answer: TR88 did not verify what the works were or prove they were Bond's responsibility, and it had also denied Bond access to the site.
records.notes: The court found the evidential basis insufficient.

records.question: What did Bond's substituted particulars emphasize about extension-of-time applications?
records.expected_answer: They relied on deemed approval if an extension request was not rejected within seven days, and on prolongation-cost claims.
records.notes: The substituted pleading sharpened the contractual EOT argument.

records.question: What was the effect of TR88 not responding to the Taking Over Certificates within 14 days?
records.expected_answer: They were deemed issued on 11 January 2022.
records.notes: The contract's deeming mechanism is expressly applied.

records.question: Why did the court give effect to the 15% per annum interest clause?
records.expected_answer: The contract fixed the rate, and the court said Article 73 of the UAE Commercial Code supports giving effect to agreed interest rates.
records.notes: The judgment expressly ties the contractual rate to Article 73.

records.question: How did the court characterize Mr Dev's delay analysis?
records.expected_answer: It was treated like a black box Primavera exercise that did not grapple with TR88's delay allegations.
records.notes: This captures the court's criticism of the expert method.

records.question: How did the court characterize Mr Bowler's 20% profit estimate?
records.expected_answer: It was described as a thumb-suck with no basis in TR88's actual profit margin.
records.notes: The court rejected the profit assumption as unsupported.

records.question: Why were Mr Dev's report costs excluded from Bond's recoverable costs?
records.expected_answer: He was Bond's employee, his report had problems, and the prolongation costs claim was abandoned.
records.notes: The costs carve-out is explained in the final order.

records.question: Why did the court accept the Fit-Out descoped works credit proposed by Bond?
records.expected_answer: Bond's figure was unopposed and TR88 offered no evidence proving a greater credit was due.
records.notes: The court relied on the lack of contrary evidence.

records.question: What warranty provider is identified for the project?
records.expected_answer: There is no information on this question in the provided documents.
records.notes: Negative: the judgment does not identify any warranty provider.
